title: Genesis: The Fall and Promise
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of genesis: the fall and promise and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study genesis: the fall and promise and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.
Review question: What did God say the offspring of the woman would do to the serpent in Genesis 3:15? Answer: crush or bruise the serpent's head, while the serpent would strike his heel.
segments.correct_answer: crush the serpent's head while the serpent strikes his heel
acceptable_variants: the woman's seed would crush the serpent
the protoevangelium — the first gospel promise of the serpent-crusher

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: Latin meaning first gospel; refers to Genesis 3:15 where God promises enmity between the serpent and the woman's offspring, read as the first messianic promise
The arrangement in Eden where Adam's obedience would secure life for himself and his descendants; broken by the fall
